review_log.note: Initial completeness review was performed inline and did not satisfy the review-driven-workflow requirement to use a reviewer subagent.
Reviewer subagent PASS: document is complete, all findings scored 2+ on clarity, testability, and completeness, with no advisories.

Initial cross-reference review passed, but the orchestrator held the stage open after identifying two unreviewed risks: over-gating all ha_config_entries_flow calls instead of only new starts, and drifting from the checklist's fingerprint file shape.
Reviewer found item 9 only partially covered: add-device-start confirmation was not traced separately, and the plan carried an unsourced dashboard/manual-option detail.
Reviewer PASS after tightening the start-only gate via tool_input/flow_id, aligning fingerprints to *.md, and making the add-device confirmation boundary explicit.
Phase review found the setup doc was not baseline-aware: preexisting entities could satisfy success and the test only checked wording removal.
Phase 1 approved after adding pre-connection baseline language, new-entity verification language, discovery handoff wording, and rerunning 7 focused tests successfully.
Phase 2 approved after adding the passive-first read-only discovery doc, discovery-first handoff guidance, and regression coverage for ordering plus the confirmation boundary.

Independent orchestrator review reran `python3 -m pytest tests/test_setup_docs.py tests/test_integrations_docs.py -v` with `PATH="$PWD/.venv/bin:$PATH"` and confirmed the Phase 2 doc changes still satisfy the issue doc, plan, and approval-boundary intent.

Added the shared Markdown fingerprint corpus for xiaomi, hue, shelly, esphome, and broadlink; updated `_discovery.md` to consume `tools/integrations/fingerprints/*.md` via shared fields; and reran `python3 -m pytest tests/test_setup_docs.py tests/test_integrations_docs.py -v` with `PATH="$PWD/.venv/bin:$PATH"` successfully (3 passed).

Tightened `tools/setup.md`, `tools/integrations/_discovery.md`, and `tools/integrations/_guide.md` so first-win completion is baseline-aware and discovered candidates still require explicit confirmation before any add-device action; reran `python3 -m pytest tests/test_setup_docs.py tests/test_integrations_docs.py -v` with `PATH="$PWD/.venv/bin:$PATH"` successfully (3 passed).

Ran the full focused regression suite `python3 -m pytest tests/test_setup_docs.py tests/test_approval_gate.py tests/test_integrations_docs.py -v` with `PATH="$PWD/.venv/bin:$PATH"`; all 9 tests passed, covering readiness, discovery, fingerprints, and the approval gate.

Validation report written to `docs/rdw-p1-5-9-validation-report.md` with file-level evidence for P1-P5 plus the full focused pytest output; all five findings validated within scope and paused item 9a remained excluded.

learnings_captured.description: Captured the non-obvious workflow lessons: start-only config-flow gating, shared Markdown fingerprint records, and baseline-aware first-win validation.
